intent: Check whether the candidate understands how synchronous exceptions work in Node.js and how to handle them without confusing them with asynchronous failures.
primaryQuestion: How do you handle synchronous errors in Node.js?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that synchronous errors are thrown during the current call stack and can be handled with try/catch around the code that may throw.

requirements.description: Explains that synchronous errors are thrown during the current call stack.
States that try/catch must wrap the code that may throw.

expectedSignals.label: Gives practical examples such as JSON.parse, input parsing, configuration loading, or synchronous filesystem calls throwing exceptions.

requirements.description: Gives examples such as JSON.parse or input parsing throwing.
Gives examples such as configuration loading or synchronous filesystem calls throwing.

expectedSignals.label: States that the handler should either recover intentionally, return a controlled response, or rethrow/delegate the error rather than silently swallowing it.

requirements.description: States the handler should recover, return a controlled response, rethrow, or delegate.
Warns against silently swallowing errors.

expectedSignals.label: Distinguishes synchronous try/catch from asynchronous error handling: a try/catch around code that schedules a timer, callback, or Promise will not catch errors that happen later unless they are awaited or forwarded.

requirements.description: Explains that try/catch around scheduling code does not catch errors thrown later.
Mentions awaiting promises or forwarding async errors to catch/handle them.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions that in HTTP frameworks such as Express, a synchronous throw inside a route or middleware can be passed into centralized error handling.

requirements.description: Mentions that Express can pass synchronous route/middleware throws to error handling.
Connects this to centralized error middleware.

expectedSignals.label: Recognizes that unexpected fatal startup errors should usually stop the process instead of leaving the service in an unknown state.

requirements.description: Recognizes unexpected fatal errors during startup/configuration.
Explains that the service should usually stop rather than run in an unknown state.

scoringHints.description: Clearly explains call-stack based exceptions, try/catch placement, safe recovery or delegation, and the boundary between sync and async errors.
Knows to use try/catch for synchronous code but gives limited detail about delegation or async boundaries.
Has heard of try/catch but cannot reason about where it must be placed or what it does not catch.
Answer would likely swallow errors, leak internals, or fail to handle real synchronous exceptions.

followUps.question: Can you give an example of Node.js code where JSON.parse throws and show where the try/catch should go?
followUps.purpose: Force concrete understanding of synchronous exception boundaries.

followUps.question: Would the same try/catch catch an error thrown later inside setTimeout? Why or why not?
followUps.purpose: Check whether the candidate separates synchronous call stack errors from asynchronous callbacks.

en.title: Handling synchronous Node.js errors
en.displayQuestion: How do you handle synchronous errors in Node.js?
en.displayIntent: Check understanding of try/catch, call stack boundaries, and safe error delegation.
guidance: Listen for try/catch around the exact synchronous operation that may throw.
Ask for a concrete example such as JSON.parse or startup config loading.
Confirm the candidate knows try/catch does not automatically catch later async failures.
commonMistakes: Using one outer try/catch for the whole server.
Swallowing errors silently.
Confusing synchronous exceptions with Promise rejections.
Returning stack traces to clients.

vi.title: Xử lý lỗi đồng bộ trong Node.js
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn xử lý synchronous errors trong Node.js như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về try/catch, ranh giới call stack và cách chuyển lỗi an toàn.
guidance: Nghe xem ứng viên đặt try/catch quanh đúng đoạn code đồng bộ có thể throw hay không.
Yêu cầu ví dụ cụ thể như JSON.parse hoặc load config lúc startup.
Xác nhận ứng viên biết try/catch không tự bắt lỗi async xảy ra sau đó.
commonMistakes: Dùng một try/catch ngoài cùng cho toàn bộ server.
Nuốt lỗi mà không log hoặc xử lý.
Nhầm synchronous exception với Promise rejection.
Trả stack trace cho client.

ja.title: Node.js の同期エラー処理
ja.displayQuestion: Node.js では synchronous error をどのように処理しますか。
ja.displayIntent: try/catch、call stack の境界、安全な error delegation の理解を確認する。
guidance: throw する可能性がある同期処理の近くに try/catch を置けるか確認する。
JSON.parse や startup config loading など具体例を求める。
try/catch が後で発生する async failure を自動的には catch しないことを確認する。
commonMistakes: server 全体を 1 つの outer try/catch で扱う。
error を silently swallow する。
synchronous exception と Promise rejection を混同する。
stack trace を client に返す。

sourceReferences.label: Node.js API: Errors
sourceReferences.note: Used for JavaScript exception and Error behavior in Node.js.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for synchronous errors thrown in Express handlers.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can safely propagate asynchronous route errors in Express instead of losing them or causing unhandled promise rejections.
primaryQuestion: How do you handle async errors in Express routes?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that async route errors should be forwarded to Express error handling rather than handled inconsistently in every route.

requirements.description: Explains that async route errors should be forwarded to Express error handling.
Avoids handling each route with inconsistent ad hoc error responses.

expectedSignals.label: For Express 4, mentions using try/catch around awaited work and calling next(err), or using a reusable asyncHandler wrapper that catches rejected promises.

requirements.description: Mentions try/catch around awaited work and calling next(err) in Express 4.
Mentions a reusable asyncHandler wrapper that catches rejected promises.

expectedSignals.label: For Express 5, mentions that rejected Promises returned from route handlers are automatically forwarded to next, while a centralized error handler is still needed.

requirements.description: Mentions Express 5 automatically forwards returned rejected promises.
States that a centralized error handler is still needed to shape/log responses.

expectedSignals.label: Understands that work started but not awaited or returned can still escape the request lifecycle and needs its own error handling.

requirements.description: Recognizes that async work not awaited or returned can escape Express request handling.
Mentions separate error handling/logging for background or detached work.

expectedSignals.label: Avoids sending a response and then also calling next(err), which can cause duplicate response or headers-sent bugs.

requirements.description: Warns against sending a response and then calling next(err).
Mentions duplicate responses or headers-already-sent bugs.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions logging the internal error and returning a safe, consistent API response through error middleware.

requirements.description: Mentions logging internal error details.
Mentions returning a safe consistent API response through error middleware.

scoringHints.description: Explains Express 4 try/catch or wrappers, Express 5 promise forwarding, centralized response formatting, and request lifecycle boundaries.
Knows to use next(err) or an async wrapper but misses one nuance such as Express version differences or unawaited work.
Knows async errors need handling but cannot describe a reliable Express pattern.
Answer would likely create unhandled promise rejections or inconsistent error responses.

followUps.question: Can you write the shape of an asyncHandler wrapper for Express 4?
followUps.purpose: Check practical ability to forward rejected promises.

followUps.question: If an async task is started in a route but not awaited or returned, will Express handle its rejection?
followUps.purpose: Probe request lifecycle and unhandled rejection boundaries.

en.title: Async errors in Express routes
en.displayQuestion: How do you handle async errors in Express routes?
en.displayIntent: Check practical async error forwarding in Express route handlers.
guidance: Listen for next(err), try/catch, or a reusable async wrapper.
Check whether the candidate knows Express 4 and Express 5 differ.
Ask what happens to unawaited background work.
commonMistakes: Assuming Express 4 catches async handler rejections automatically.
Logging without responding or delegating.
Calling next() instead of next(err).
Ignoring unawaited async work.

vi.title: Lỗi async trong route Express
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn xử lý async errors trong Express routes như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra khả năng forward lỗi async trong Express route handler.
guidance: Nghe các pattern như next(err), try/catch hoặc async wrapper tái sử dụng.
Kiểm tra ứng viên có biết Express 4 và Express 5 khác nhau hay không.
Hỏi điều gì xảy ra với async work không await.
commonMistakes: Tưởng Express 4 tự bắt rejected Promise từ async handler.
Chỉ log mà không trả response hoặc delegate.
Gọi next() thay vì next(err).
Bỏ qua async work không được await.

ja.title: Express route の async error
ja.displayQuestion: Express route では async error をどのように処理しますか。
ja.displayIntent: Express route handler で async error を適切に forward できるか確認する。
guidance: next(err)、try/catch、再利用可能な async wrapper に触れるか確認する。
Express 4 と Express 5 の違いを知っているか見る。
await されない background work がどうなるか質問する。
commonMistakes: Express 4 が async handler rejection を自動的に catch すると考える。
log だけして response も delegation もしない。
next(err) ではなく next() を呼ぶ。
await されない async work を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for next(err), async route error behavior, and Express 5 Promise forwarding.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands the role of Express error-handling middleware as a centralized error response and logging point.

expectedSignals.label: Defines Express error-handling middleware as middleware used on the error path, usually registered after routes and normal middleware.

requirements.description: Defines Express error middleware as middleware used on the error path.
Mentions registering it after routes and normal middleware.

expectedSignals.label: States that it has the special signature (err, req, res, next), which distinguishes it from normal middleware.

requirements.description: States the (err, req, res, next) signature.
Explains that the four-argument signature distinguishes it from normal middleware.

expectedSignals.label: Explains that it receives errors from thrown synchronous exceptions, next(err), and in Express 5 returned rejected Promises.

requirements.description: Mentions thrown synchronous exceptions and next(err) as sources.
Mentions returned rejected Promises in Express 5.

expectedSignals.label: Describes practical responsibilities: log internal details, map known errors to HTTP status codes, return a safe response shape, and delegate when needed.

requirements.description: Mentions logging internal details and mapping known errors to status codes.
Mentions returning a safe response shape and delegating when needed.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions that order matters because it should be registered after routes it is meant to handle.

requirements.description: States that error handler registration order matters.
Explains it should be after routes it is meant to handle.

expectedSignals.label: Understands that it should not expose stack traces or sensitive internals in production responses.

requirements.description: Warns not to expose stack traces in production responses.
Warns not to expose sensitive internals, secrets, SQL, or implementation details.

scoringHints.description: Explains the error-path role, signature, registration order, forwarding sources, response mapping, and safe production behavior.
Understands that it centralizes Express errors but misses one practical detail such as ordering or safe response shaping.
Has seen error middleware but cannot clearly explain when it runs or what it should do.
Answer confuses error middleware with normal middleware or would leave requests unresolved.

followUps.question: Where would you register a global Express error handler relative to your routes?
followUps.purpose: Check middleware ordering knowledge.

followUps.question: What should the error middleware do if res.headersSent is already true?
followUps.purpose: Probe response lifecycle awareness at a junior-friendly level.

en.title: Express error middleware
en.displayIntent: Check the candidate's understanding of centralized Express error handling.
guidance: Listen for the err, req, res, next signature.
Confirm it is registered after routes.
Look for logging, HTTP mapping, and safe response formatting.
commonMistakes: Using normal middleware signature.
Thinking it runs for successful requests.
Registering it in the wrong order.
Returning raw errors to clients.

vi.title: Error middleware trong Express
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về xử lý lỗi tập trung trong Express.
guidance: Nghe chữ ký err, req, res, next.
Xác nhận middleware này được đăng ký sau routes.
Tìm ý về logging, mapping HTTP status và response an toàn.
commonMistakes: Dùng chữ ký middleware thường.
Tưởng nó chạy cho request thành công.
Đăng ký sai thứ tự.
Trả raw error cho client.

ja.title: Express error middleware
ja.displayQuestion: Express error-handling middleware とは何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: Express の centralized error handling を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: err, req, res, next の signature に触れるか確認する。
routes の後に登録することを確認する。
logging、HTTP mapping、安全な response formatting を見る。
commonMistakes: 通常 middleware の signature を使う。
成功 request でも実行されると思う。
登録順を間違える。
raw error を client に返す。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for Express error-handling middleware behavior and signature.

intent: Verify whether the candidate understands why Express error middleware uses a four-argument err-first function shape.
primaryQuestion: Why does error middleware use (err, req, res, next)?

expectedSignals.label: States that Express recognizes error-handling middleware by the declared four-argument signature.

requirements.description: States that Express recognizes error middleware by its declared four arguments.
Mentions that the function shape matters at runtime.

expectedSignals.label: Explains that err is the error object or rejected value being passed through the middleware chain.

requirements.description: Explains err as the error object or rejected value.
Connects err to being passed through the Express error middleware chain.

expectedSignals.label: Contrasts it with normal middleware, which usually uses (req, res, next) and handles normal request flow.

requirements.description: Contrasts (err, req, res, next) with normal (req, res, next).
Explains that normal middleware handles normal request flow while error middleware handles error flow.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions that req and res are still needed to inspect request context and send an appropriate HTTP response.

requirements.description: Mentions req is used to inspect request context.
Mentions res is used to send an appropriate HTTP response.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions that next is still needed to delegate to the next error handler or the default handler, especially when headers are already sent.

requirements.description: Mentions next can delegate to the next error handler or default handler.
Mentions delegating when headers have already been sent.

expectedSignals.label: Understands that removing an unused parameter can change how Express treats the middleware at runtime.

requirements.description: Warns that removing an apparently unused parameter can change the declared function arity.
Connects function arity to how Express classifies middleware.

scoringHints.description: Explains Express arity-based detection, err-first flow, the role of req/res/next, and why all parameters should be preserved.
Knows the signature and general purpose but misses arity or delegation nuance.
Can repeat the signature but cannot explain why Express needs it.
Answer would cause Express to treat the function as normal middleware or lose error context.

followUps.question: How is (err, req, res, next) different from (req, res, next) in the middleware chain?
followUps.purpose: Force direct contrast with normal middleware.

en.title: Express error handler signature
en.displayQuestion: Why does error middleware use (err, req, res, next)?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate understands the err-first four-parameter contract.
guidance: Listen for Express detecting error handlers by four arguments.
Ask what err contains.
Confirm the candidate knows next may still be needed.
commonMistakes: Treating the signature as cosmetic.
Putting err in the wrong position.
Removing next without understanding the consequence.
Relying only on TypeScript types.

vi.title: Chữ ký error handler Express
vi.displayQuestion: Tại sao error middleware dùng (err, req, res, next)?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên hiểu contract bốn tham số với err đứng đầu.
guidance: Nghe ý Express nhận diện error handler qua bốn tham số.
Hỏi err chứa gì.
Xác nhận ứng viên biết next vẫn có thể cần dùng.
commonMistakes: Xem chữ ký chỉ là style.
Đặt err sai vị trí.
Xóa next mà không hiểu hậu quả.
Chỉ dựa vào type TypeScript.

ja.title: Express error handler signature
ja.displayQuestion: error middleware はなぜ (err, req, res, next) を使いますか。
ja.displayIntent: err-first の 4-argument contract を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: Express が 4 arguments で error handler を識別することを確認する。
err に何が入るか質問する。
next がまだ必要になり得ることを確認する。
commonMistakes: signature を見た目だけの style と考える。
err の位置を間違える。
意味を理解せず next を削除する。
TypeScript type だけに頼る。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for the four-argument error-handling middleware contract.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands unhandled Promise rejections and why they are a reliability problem in Node.js services.
primaryQuestion: What is an unhandled promise rejection?

expectedSignals.label: Defines it as a Promise that rejects without a rejection handler such as catch or an awaited try/catch being attached in time.

requirements.description: Defines an unhandled rejection as a Promise rejection without a handler attached in time.
Mentions catch or try/catch around await as ways to handle rejection.

expectedSignals.label: Explains common causes: forgetting await, forgetting return, missing catch, or starting async work without observing the Promise.

requirements.description: Mentions forgetting to await or return a Promise.
Mentions missing catch or starting async work without observing the Promise.

expectedSignals.label: Connects it to real service risk: failed operations may not produce a proper HTTP response, logs may be incomplete, and the process may terminate depending on Node.js behavior and configuration.

requirements.description: Explains failed operations may not produce proper HTTP responses or logs.
Mentions process behavior/termination depending on Node.js version or configuration.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions that async functions return Promises, so throwing inside an async function becomes a rejection.

requirements.description: States that async functions return Promises.
Explains that throwing inside an async function becomes a Promise rejection.

expectedSignals.label: Suggests prevention patterns such as always await or return Promises, use catch, use Express async error handling, and test failure paths.

requirements.description: Mentions awaiting, returning, or catching Promises.
Mentions Express async error handling and testing failure paths.

expectedSignals.label: Understands that process-level unhandledRejection handlers are for logging and controlled shutdown policy, not a substitute for local error handling.

requirements.description: Describes process-level unhandledRejection as logging/controlled shutdown policy.
States it is not a substitute for request-level or local error handling.

scoringHints.description: Defines unhandled rejection accurately, explains async function behavior, gives common causes, and proposes practical prevention.
Understands rejected Promises need catch or await but misses process-level or framework nuance.
Has heard the term but cannot identify how unhandled rejections happen in real code.
Answer would leave rejected Promises unobserved or normalize process-level failure handling.

followUps.question: What happens if you call an async function but forget to await or return its Promise?
followUps.purpose: Check practical cause of unhandled rejections.

followUps.question: Why is a process-level unhandledRejection listener not enough for normal route error handling?
followUps.purpose: Separate crash logging policy from request-level control flow.

en.title: Unhandled Promise rejections
en.displayQuestion: What is an unhandled promise rejection?
en.displayIntent: Check Promise rejection fundamentals and production risk awareness.
guidance: Listen for missing catch or missing awaited try/catch.
Ask about async functions returning Promises.
Check whether the candidate treats process-level handlers as last-resort handling.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to await a Promise.
Assuming try/catch works without await.
Using unhandledRejection as normal flow.
Ignoring process termination risk.

vi.title: Unhandled Promise rejection
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra nền tảng Promise rejection và nhận thức rủi ro production.
guidance: Nghe ý thiếu catch hoặc thiếu awaited try/catch.
Hỏi về việc async function trả về Promise.
Kiểm tra ứng viên có xem process-level handler là lớp cuối cùng hay không.
commonMistakes: Quên await Promise.
Tưởng try/catch hoạt động dù không await.
Dùng unhandledRejection như luồng xử lý bình thường.
Bỏ qua rủi ro process bị terminate.

ja.title: Unhandled Promise rejection
ja.displayQuestion: unhandled promise rejection とは何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: Promise rejection の基礎と production risk の認識を確認する。
guidance: catch がない、または await された try/catch がないことを説明できるか確認する。
async function が Promise を返すことを質問する。
process-level handler を最後の防衛線として扱うか確認する。
commonMistakes: Promise を await し忘れる。
await なしでも try/catch が効くと思う。
unhandledRejection を通常の処理フローに使う。
process termination risk を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: Node.js API: process unhandledRejection
sourceReferences.note: Used for Node.js unhandled Promise rejection behavior.

sourceReferences.label: MDN: Promise catch
sourceReferences.note: Used for Promise rejection handling semantics.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands why production APIs should separate internal diagnostic errors from public error responses.
primaryQuestion: Why should production APIs not expose stack traces to users?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that stack traces reveal internal file paths, framework details, function names, package versions, query fragments, or implementation structure.

requirements.description: Mentions file paths, framework details, function names, package versions, query fragments, or implementation structure.
Explains that stack traces reveal implementation details.

expectedSignals.label: Connects leaked internals to security risk because attackers can use the information to target known vulnerabilities or map the system.

requirements.description: Connects leaked internals to attacker reconnaissance or system mapping.
Mentions targeting known vulnerabilities based on revealed libraries/versions.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions that stack traces may accidentally include sensitive data such as tokens, database details, environment values, or user input.

requirements.description: Mentions tokens, database details, environment values, or user input appearing in errors.
Explains why that information should not be returned to clients.

expectedSignals.label: States that production responses should return safe messages and stable error codes while detailed stack traces go to protected logs or observability tools.

requirements.description: States production responses should use safe messages and stable error codes.
States detailed stack traces belong in protected logs or observability tools.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions environment-aware behavior: verbose errors may be acceptable in local development but not in production.

requirements.description: Distinguishes local development from production error verbosity.
Mentions environment-aware behavior or NODE_ENV-like configuration.

expectedSignals.label: Understands that hiding stack traces does not mean hiding all errors; clients still need useful status codes and request correlation IDs.

requirements.description: States clients should still receive useful HTTP status codes.
Mentions request ID, correlation ID, or trace ID for support/debugging.

scoringHints.description: Explains information disclosure risks, sensitive data leakage, environment-specific behavior, internal logging, and safe client response design.
Knows not to expose stack traces but gives limited detail about what to return instead.
Understands it is bad but cannot explain concrete risks or safe alternatives.
Answer would leak internals or remove useful diagnostics altogether.

followUps.question: What information would you return to the client, and what would you keep only in logs?
followUps.purpose: Check separation between public error contract and internal diagnostics.

en.title: Stack traces in production APIs
en.displayQuestion: Why should production APIs not expose stack traces to users?
en.displayIntent: Check information disclosure and safe error response awareness.
guidance: Listen for information disclosure and sensitive data leakage.
Confirm detailed errors still go to protected logs.
Ask what a safe client error response should contain.
commonMistakes: Returning err.stack to clients.
Removing all logging.
Thinking stack traces are harmless.
Giving clients no useful error code or request ID.

vi.title: Stack trace trong production API
vi.displayQuestion: Tại sao production APIs không nên expose stack traces cho users?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra nhận thức về information disclosure và response lỗi an toàn.
guidance: Nghe ý lộ thông tin nội bộ và dữ liệu nhạy cảm.
Xác nhận lỗi chi tiết vẫn được ghi vào log được bảo vệ.
Hỏi response lỗi an toàn cho client nên gồm gì.
commonMistakes: Trả err.stack cho client.
Tắt toàn bộ logging.
Nghĩ stack trace vô hại.
Không cung cấp error code hoặc request ID hữu ích.

ja.title: production API の stack trace
ja.displayQuestion: production API で stack trace を user に見せるべきではないのはなぜですか。
ja.displayIntent: information disclosure と安全な error response の理解を確認する。
guidance: 内部情報や sensitive data の漏洩に触れるか確認する。
詳細な error は protected logs に残すことを確認する。
client 向けの安全な error response に何を含めるか質問する。
commonMistakes: err.stack を client に返す。
logging をすべて無効にする。
stack trace は無害だと考える。
error code や request ID を返さない。

sourceReferences.label: OWASP Top 10: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
sourceReferences.note: Used for logging and monitoring risk framing.

sourceReferences.label: OWASP Error Handling Cheat Sheet
sourceReferences.note: Used for safe error handling and information disclosure guidance.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands process.env as Node.js runtime configuration and its practical limitations.

expectedSignals.label: Explains that process.env exposes environment variables available to the Node.js process at runtime.

requirements.description: Explains that process.env exposes environment variables.
Mentions variables available to the current Node.js process at runtime.

expectedSignals.label: Gives examples such as PORT, NODE_ENV, database URLs, feature flags, API endpoints, and secret references.

requirements.description: Gives examples such as PORT, NODE_ENV, database URLs, or API endpoints.
Mentions feature flags or secret references.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions that values from process.env are strings or undefined, so the application should parse and validate numbers, booleans, URLs, and required values.

requirements.description: States process.env values are strings or undefined.
Mentions parsing and validating numbers, booleans, URLs, and required values.

expectedSignals.label: Understands that process.env is commonly used to keep environment-specific configuration out of source code.

requirements.description: Explains process.env is used for environment-specific configuration.
Mentions keeping deployment-specific config out of source code.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions that secrets may be injected through environment variables or secret managers, but should still be protected and not logged.

requirements.description: Mentions injecting secrets through env vars or secret managers.
Warns that secrets should be protected and not logged.

expectedSignals.label: Recognizes that reading process.env directly everywhere can make configuration inconsistent; many apps centralize validation in a config module.

requirements.description: Recognizes that reading process.env directly everywhere can become inconsistent.
Mentions centralizing validation in a config module or startup step.

scoringHints.description: Explains runtime environment configuration, string parsing, validation, environment separation, and safe secret handling.
Understands process.env for configuration but misses typing, validation, or secret-handling nuance.
Can name environment variables but cannot describe safe usage in a Node.js app.
Answer would lead to insecure or unreliable runtime configuration.

followUps.question: If process.env.PORT is set to "3000", what type is that value and what should the app do before using it?
followUps.purpose: Check string typing and parsing awareness.

followUps.question: Where would you validate required environment variables in a Node.js service?
followUps.purpose: Probe startup configuration discipline.

en.title: Using process.env
en.displayIntent: Check runtime configuration basics in Node.js.
guidance: Listen for environment-specific runtime configuration.
Check whether values are parsed and validated.
Ask how secrets are protected and kept out of logs.
commonMistakes: Treating process.env as a secret vault.
Forgetting all values are strings or undefined.
Logging environment variables.
Using unsafe hardcoded defaults.

vi.title: Sử dụng process.env
vi.displayQuestion: process.env được dùng để làm gì?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra nền tảng runtime configuration trong Node.js.
guidance: Nghe ý cấu hình runtime theo môi trường.
Kiểm tra việc parse và validate giá trị.
Hỏi cách bảo vệ secret và tránh ghi vào log.
commonMistakes: Xem process.env là secret vault.
Quên mọi giá trị là string hoặc undefined.
Log environment variables.
Dùng default hardcoded không an toàn.

ja.title: process.env の利用
ja.displayQuestion: process.env は何に使いますか。
ja.displayIntent: Node.js の runtime configuration の基礎を確認する。
guidance: environment ごとの runtime configuration に触れるか確認する。
値を parse/validate するか確認する。
secret の保護と log への出力を避けることを質問する。
commonMistakes: process.env を secret vault と考える。
値がすべて string または undefined であることを忘れる。
environment variables を log する。
安全でない hardcoded default を使う。

sourceReferences.label: Node.js API: process.env
sourceReferences.note: Used for process.env runtime behavior.

sourceReferences.label: The Twelve-Factor App: Config
sourceReferences.note: Used for environment-based configuration principles.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands the security and operational risks of hardcoded secrets in Node.js applications.
primaryQuestion: Why should secrets and API keys not be hardcoded?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that hardcoded secrets can be committed to source control, copied into logs, shared in pull requests, or included in built artifacts.

requirements.description: Mentions exposure through source control, pull requests, built artifacts, or logs.
Explains that hardcoding spreads secrets beyond the runtime environment.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions that source repositories often have many readers and long history, so removing a committed secret later does not fully undo exposure.

requirements.description: Mentions repository readers, forks, caches, or long commit history.
Explains that removing a committed secret does not fully undo exposure.

expectedSignals.label: Connects hardcoded secrets to weak environment separation because local, test, staging, and production may accidentally share credentials.

requirements.description: Explains that hardcoding weakens separation between environments.
Mentions accidental credential sharing across local, test, staging, or production.

expectedSignals.label: Explains that rotation becomes harder because changing the secret requires code changes, rebuilds, redeploys, and cleanup across versions.

requirements.description: Explains that secret rotation becomes harder.
Mentions code changes, rebuilds, redeploys, or cleanup across versions.

expectedSignals.label: Suggests safer approaches such as environment variables, secret managers, deployment platform secrets, restricted IAM roles, and runtime injection.

requirements.description: Mentions environment variables, secret managers, or deployment platform secrets.
Mentions restricted IAM roles or runtime injection.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions least privilege and monitoring: secrets should have limited scope, be rotated when exposed, and not be logged.

requirements.description: Mentions least privilege or limiting secret scope.
Mentions rotating exposed secrets, monitoring usage, and not logging them.

scoringHints.description: Explains repository exposure, history persistence, environment separation, rotation cost, safer storage, least privilege, and logging discipline.
Understands hardcoding is insecure but misses rotation, repo history, or environment separation.
Repeats that secrets should not be hardcoded but cannot explain practical consequences.
Answer would normalize secret leakage or unsafe credential handling.

followUps.question: Where would you store database credentials for staging and production in a deployment pipeline?
followUps.purpose: Probe practical secret delivery approach.

en.title: Avoid hardcoded secrets
en.displayQuestion: Why should secrets and API keys not be hardcoded?
guidance: Listen for source control and artifact exposure.
Ask about rotation after accidental commit.
Check whether the candidate separates environments and avoids logging secrets.
commonMistakes: Trusting private repos as secret storage.
Reusing one secret across environments.
Logging secrets for debugging.
Deleting a leaked secret without rotating it.

vi.title: Tránh hardcode secret
vi.displayQuestion: Tại sao không nên hardcode secrets và API keys?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra nhận thức thực tế về quản lý secret.
guidance: Nghe ý lộ qua source control và build artifact.
Hỏi về rotation sau khi lỡ commit.
Kiểm tra việc tách môi trường và không log secret.
commonMistakes: Tin private repo là nơi lưu secret an toàn.
Dùng chung một secret cho nhiều môi trường.
Log secret để debug.
Xóa secret đã leak nhưng không rotate.

ja.title: hardcoded secrets を避ける
ja.displayQuestion: secrets や API keys を hardcode すべきではないのはなぜですか。
ja.displayIntent: 実務的な secret management の理解を確認する。
guidance: source control や artifact からの漏洩に触れるか確認する。
誤って commit した後の rotation について質問する。
environment separation と secret を log しないことを確認する。
commonMistakes: private repo を secret storage として信頼する。
複数 environment で同じ secret を再利用する。
debug のため secret を log する。
leak した secret を削除するだけで rotate しない。

sourceReferences.label: OWASP Secrets Management Cheat Sheet
sourceReferences.note: Used for secret lifecycle, access control, rotation, and storage principles.

intent: Check whether the candidate knows that missing required configuration should be detected early and fail safely instead of causing hidden runtime errors.
primaryQuestion: What should happen if a required environment variable is missing?

expectedSignals.label: States that the application should fail fast during startup or configuration loading when a required variable is missing.

requirements.description: States that the app should fail fast when required config is missing.
Places the check during startup or configuration loading.

expectedSignals.label: Explains that the error should be clear for operators or developers, naming the missing configuration key without printing sensitive values.

requirements.description: Mentions naming the missing configuration key in the error.
Warns not to print sensitive values.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions using a central config validation step or schema to check required variables and parse types before the app starts serving traffic.

requirements.description: Mentions central config validation or a schema.
Mentions checking required vars and parsing types before serving traffic.

expectedSignals.label: Rejects unsafe fallback defaults for production secrets, database URLs, signing keys, or payment credentials.

requirements.description: Rejects unsafe fallback defaults for secrets.
Mentions database URLs, signing keys, or payment credentials as unsafe to default silently.

expectedSignals.label: Connects fail-fast behavior to safer deployments: health checks should fail, the release should not accept traffic, and misconfiguration should be visible quickly.

requirements.description: Explains that health checks/startup should fail on missing config.
Explains the release should not accept traffic while misconfigured.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions that tests or CI can validate required configuration for each environment where practical.

requirements.description: Mentions tests or CI checking required configuration.
Mentions validating expected config per environment where practical.

scoringHints.description: Explains fail-fast startup validation, clear safe diagnostics, type parsing, no insecure defaults, health check implications, and CI/test support.
Knows the app should not continue but misses validation centralization or safe diagnostics.
Mentions checking env vars but accepts late runtime failure or unsafe defaults.
Answer would allow misconfigured production services to run unsafely.

followUps.question: Would you validate environment variables inside each route or once at startup? Why?
followUps.purpose: Probe configuration design and fail-fast reasoning.

en.title: Missing required environment variables
en.displayQuestion: What should happen if a required environment variable is missing?
guidance: Listen for startup validation and process failure.
Check that errors are useful but do not leak secret values.
Ask about unsafe production defaults.
commonMistakes: Silently using fallback secrets.
Starting the app and failing later.
Logging secret values.
Treating required config as optional.

vi.title: Thiếu biến môi trường bắt buộc
vi.displayQuestion: Nếu thiếu một required environment variable thì nên xảy ra điều gì?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hành vi validate config fail-fast.
guidance: Nghe ý validate lúc startup và dừng process.
Kiểm tra lỗi có hữu ích nhưng không leak secret value.
Hỏi về default production không an toàn.
commonMistakes: Âm thầm dùng fallback secret.
Cho app start rồi lỗi sau.
Log giá trị secret.
Xem required config là optional.

ja.title: 必須 environment variable の欠落
ja.displayQuestion: required environment variable が missing の場合、何が起きるべきですか。
ja.displayIntent: fail-fast な configuration validation を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: startup validation と process failure に触れるか確認する。
error が有用だが secret value を漏らさないことを確認する。
安全でない production default について質問する。
commonMistakes: fallback secret を silently use する。
app を起動して後で失敗させる。
secret value を log する。
required config を optional として扱う。

sourceReferences.label: The Twelve-Factor App: Config
sourceReferences.note: Used for environment-based configuration principles.

sourceReferences.label: Node.js API: process.env
sourceReferences.note: Used for Node.js environment variable behavior.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands that all client-controlled input can be malicious, malformed, or inconsistent with business rules.
primaryQuestion: Why should user input never be trusted?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that clients can be modified, requests can be crafted manually, and frontend validation can be bypassed.

requirements.description: Explains that clients/frontends can be modified.
Mentions crafted requests and bypassed frontend validation.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions risks such as SQL or NoSQL injection, command injection, XSS, path traversal, mass assignment, authentication bypass, and resource exhaustion.

requirements.description: Mentions SQL/NoSQL injection, command injection, or XSS.
Mentions path traversal, mass assignment, auth bypass, or resource exhaustion.

expectedSignals.label: States that backend services must validate and authorize input even if the frontend already validates it.

requirements.description: States backend services must validate input.
States backend services must enforce authorization even if the frontend validates.

expectedSignals.label: Distinguishes validation from sanitization and escaping: validate shape and allowed values, sanitize or normalize where appropriate, and escape output in the right context.

requirements.description: Distinguishes validation from sanitization/normalization and output escaping.
Explains escaping output in the correct context such as HTML.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions using parameterized queries, allowlists, schemas, size limits, and safe parsers rather than string concatenation.

requirements.description: Mentions parameterized queries or allowlists.
Mentions schemas, size limits, safe parsers, and avoiding string concatenation.

expectedSignals.label: Recognizes that user identity, role, ownership, and permissions must be checked server-side and cannot be trusted from client-provided fields.

requirements.description: Mentions server-side checks for identity, roles, ownership, or permissions.
Warns not to trust client-provided role/owner/permission fields.

scoringHints.description: Explains bypassable clients, concrete attack classes, backend validation and authorization, allowlists, safe APIs, and context-specific escaping.
Understands input can be malicious and should be validated but misses some security mechanisms or authorization concerns.
Gives generic advice but cannot name realistic risks or backend controls.
Answer would trust client-controlled data or rely only on frontend validation.

followUps.question: What is the difference between validating a string and escaping it for HTML output?
followUps.purpose: Probe validation versus output encoding distinction.

en.title: Never trust user input
en.displayQuestion: Why should user input never be trusted?
en.displayIntent: Check basic secure backend input handling.
guidance: Listen for bypassable clients and crafted requests.
Ask for concrete attack examples.
Check server-side validation, authorization, and safe API usage.
commonMistakes: Trusting the frontend.
Relying only on client-side types or form validation.
Concatenating input into SQL or commands.
Trusting client-sent roles or ownership.

vi.title: Không tin user input
vi.displayQuestion: Tại sao không bao giờ nên trust user input?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra nền tảng xử lý input an toàn ở backend.
guidance: Nghe ý client có thể bị bypass và request có thể tự craft.
Yêu cầu ví dụ tấn công cụ thể.
Kiểm tra validation, authorization và safe API ở server-side.
commonMistakes: Tin frontend.
Chỉ dựa vào type hoặc form validation phía client.
Nối chuỗi input vào SQL hoặc command.
Tin role hoặc ownership client gửi lên.

ja.title: user input を信頼しない
ja.displayQuestion: user input を決して信頼すべきではないのはなぜですか。
ja.displayIntent: backend における安全な input handling の基礎を確認する。
guidance: client は bypass 可能で crafted request を送れることに触れるか確認する。
具体的な attack example を求める。
server-side validation、authorization、安全な API 利用を確認する。
commonMistakes: frontend を信頼する。
client-side type や form validation だけに頼る。
input を SQL や command に文字列連結する。
client が送った role や ownership を信頼する。

sourceReferences.label: OWASP Input Validation Cheat Sheet
sourceReferences.note: Used for server-side validation and allowlist principles.

sourceReferences.label: OWASP Top 10: Injection
sourceReferences.note: Used for injection risk framing.

intent: Check whether the candidate can describe basic request validation for Node.js APIs in a practical and implementation-ready way.

expectedSignals.label: Defines request validation as checking incoming body, query, params, headers, and sometimes files before business logic uses them.

requirements.description: Defines validation across body, query, params, headers, and sometimes files.
States validation happens before business logic uses the data.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions checking required fields, types, formats, length, ranges, enum values, allowed keys, and nested object shapes.

requirements.description: Mentions required fields, types, formats, length, or ranges.
Mentions enum values, allowed keys, or nested object shapes.

expectedSignals.label: Explains that invalid requests should receive a clear 400-level response with safe validation details rather than causing internal errors.

requirements.description: Mentions returning a clear 400-level response for invalid requests.
Explains invalid input should not cause uncaught internal errors.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions using validation libraries or schemas such as Zod, Joi, Yup, express-validator, JSON Schema, or framework-provided pipes where appropriate.

requirements.description: Mentions libraries such as Zod, Joi, Yup, JSON Schema, or express-validator.
Mentions framework-provided pipes or validators where appropriate.

expectedSignals.label: Understands that validation should happen on the server even when the frontend already validates.

requirements.description: States validation must happen server-side.
Explains frontend validation can be bypassed and is not sufficient.

expectedSignals.label: Distinguishes validation from authorization and business rules: validation checks shape and allowed values, while authorization checks permission and ownership.

requirements.description: Explains validation checks shape, types, and allowed values.
Explains authorization checks permissions, ownership, or policy.

scoringHints.description: Covers request locations, required fields, types, ranges, allowed values, safe 400 responses, schema tools, server-side validation, and boundaries with authorization.
Understands checking input shape and returning 400 but misses schema tools or validation versus authorization boundaries.
Gives a shallow answer such as checking if fields exist without types, ranges, or safe errors.
Answer would allow malformed or unsafe input into core logic.

followUps.question: For a createUser endpoint, what would you validate for email, password, and age?
followUps.purpose: Force concrete field-level validation reasoning.

followUps.question: If validation passes, does that mean the user is allowed to perform the action?
followUps.purpose: Check distinction between validation and authorization.

en.title: Basic request validation
guidance: Listen for body, query, params, headers, and files.
Ask about types, required fields, formats, ranges, and allowed values.
Confirm invalid input gets a safe 400-level response.
commonMistakes: Only checking req.body exists.
Trusting frontend validation.
Letting invalid input reach database code.
Confusing validation with authorization.

vi.title: Request validation cơ bản
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra nền tảng validation API thực tế.
guidance: Nghe các phần body, query, params, headers và files.
Hỏi về type, required fields, format, range và allowed values.
Xác nhận input sai được trả response 400 an toàn.
commonMistakes: Chỉ kiểm tra req.body tồn tại.
Tin validation phía frontend.
Để input sai đi vào database code.
Nhầm validation với authorization.

ja.title: 基本的な request validation
ja.displayQuestion: basic request validation とは何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: 実務的な API validation の基礎を確認する。
guidance: body、query、params、headers、files に触れるか確認する。
type、required fields、format、range、allowed values を質問する。
invalid input に安全な 400 系 response を返すことを確認する。
commonMistakes: req.body の存在だけを確認する。
frontend validation を信頼する。
invalid input を database code まで通す。
validation と authorization を混同する。

sourceReferences.label: OWASP Input Validation Cheat Sheet
sourceReferences.note: Used for input validation practices and allowlist framing.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands basic password storage safety and can distinguish hashing from encryption or plain storage.
primaryQuestion: Why should passwords be hashed instead of stored directly?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that plain-text password storage exposes every user's password immediately if the database, logs, or backups are breached.

requirements.description: Explains that plaintext storage exposes user passwords immediately in a breach.
Mentions exposure through databases, logs, or backups.

expectedSignals.label: Defines password hashing as a one-way transformation used to verify a password without storing the original password.

requirements.description: Defines password hashing as a one-way transformation.
Explains verification without storing the original password.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions that password hashes should use a slow, salted, password-specific algorithm such as Argon2id, bcrypt, or scrypt rather than fast general hashes like SHA-256 alone.

requirements.description: Mentions Argon2id, bcrypt, or scrypt.
Warns against using fast general hashes such as SHA-256 alone for passwords.

expectedSignals.label: Explains that a unique salt prevents identical passwords from having identical hashes and makes precomputed attacks harder.

requirements.description: Explains that unique salts prevent identical passwords from producing identical hashes.
Mentions making rainbow-table or precomputed attacks harder.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions password verification by hashing the submitted password with the stored parameters and comparing the result safely.

requirements.description: Describes verifying by hashing the submitted password with stored parameters.
Mentions comparing the result safely using the library or constant-time style comparison where applicable.

expectedSignals.label: Recognizes that passwords should not be logged, emailed, returned by APIs, or stored reversibly unless there is a very specific non-password credential use case.

requirements.description: States passwords should not be logged, emailed, or returned by APIs.
Warns against storing passwords reversibly for normal password use cases.

scoringHints.description: Explains breach impact, one-way verification, slow salted password hashing, appropriate algorithms, salts, safe comparison, and no password logging.
Knows passwords should be hashed and salted but misses algorithm selection or verification details.
Knows plain text is bad but confuses hashing, encryption, and fast checksums.
Answer would store recoverable or weakly protected passwords.

followUps.question: If a user forgets their password, should the system be able to show it to them? What should happen instead?
followUps.purpose: Probe one-way storage and reset flow reasoning.

en.title: Password hashing
en.displayQuestion: Why should passwords be hashed instead of stored directly?
guidance: Listen for breach impact of plain-text storage.
Check one-way, salted, slow password hashing.
Ask about bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2id rather than fast hashes.
commonMistakes: Storing plain-text passwords.
Using reversible encryption as the main approach.
Using fast unsalted hashes.
Logging submitted passwords.

vi.title: Hash mật khẩu
vi.displayQuestion: Tại sao password nên được hash thay vì lưu trực tiếp?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra nền tảng lưu password an toàn.
guidance: Nghe ý rủi ro khi database plain-text bị leak.
Kiểm tra one-way, salted, slow password hashing.
Hỏi về bcrypt, scrypt hoặc Argon2id thay vì fast hash.
commonMistakes: Lưu password plain text.
Dùng reversible encryption làm cách chính.
Dùng fast hash không salt.
Log password user nhập.

ja.title: password hashing
ja.displayQuestion: password は直接保存せず hash すべきなのはなぜですか。
ja.displayIntent: 安全な password storage の基礎を確認する。
guidance: plain-text storage が breach 時に与える影響に触れるか確認する。
one-way、salted、slow password hashing を確認する。
fast hash ではなく bcrypt、scrypt、Argon2id に触れるか質問する。
commonMistakes: plain-text password を保存する。
reversible encryption を主な方法にする。
fast unsalted hash を使う。
入力された password を log する。

sourceReferences.label: OWASP Password Storage Cheat Sheet
sourceReferences.note: Used for password hashing algorithms, salts, and work factors.

intent: Check whether the candidate can clearly distinguish authentication from authorization and apply both in backend request handling.
primaryQuestion: What is the difference between authentication and authorization?

expectedSignals.label: Defines authentication as verifying who the user or client is, such as login with password, session cookie, JWT, API key, or OAuth token.

requirements.description: Defines authentication as verifying who the user/client is.
Names login/password, session cookie, JWT, API key, or OAuth token.

expectedSignals.label: Defines authorization as deciding what that authenticated identity is allowed to do or access.

requirements.description: Defines authorization as deciding what an identity may do or access.
Connects authorization decisions to the authenticated identity.

expectedSignals.label: Gives a practical example: a logged-in user is authenticated, but may not be authorized to delete another user's resource or access an admin endpoint.

requirements.description: Uses an example where a logged-in user is authenticated but not permitted.
Mentions another user's resource, ownership, or admin endpoint.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions that authorization checks should happen server-side for protected operations and should not rely on hidden frontend buttons.

requirements.description: States authorization checks must happen server-side.
Explains hiding frontend UI is not sufficient authorization.

expectedSignals.label: Recognizes that authentication alone is not enough; every sensitive route should still enforce permissions, roles, ownership, or policy checks.

requirements.description: States that being authenticated does not imply every action is allowed.
Mentions permissions, roles, ownership, or policy checks.

expectedSignals.label: May mention common HTTP semantics: 401 for unauthenticated or invalid credentials and 403 for authenticated but not allowed.

requirements.description: Maps 401 to unauthenticated or invalid credentials.
Maps 403 to authenticated but not allowed.

scoringHints.description: Clearly separates identity verification from permission checks, gives examples, mentions server-side enforcement, and understands 401 versus 403.
Explains the basic difference but misses frontend bypass, ownership checks, or HTTP status nuance.
Has a rough idea but mixes login state with permission to perform actions.
Answer would allow authenticated users to bypass authorization controls.

followUps.question: A user is logged in and tries to edit another user's profile. Which part is authentication and which part is authorization?
followUps.purpose: Force a concrete identity versus permission example.

followUps.question: Why is hiding an admin button in the UI not enough authorization?
followUps.purpose: Check server-side enforcement awareness.

en.title: Authentication versus authorization
en.displayQuestion: What is the difference between authentication and authorization?
guidance: Listen for who you are versus what you can do.
Ask for a route-level example.
Confirm authorization is enforced on the server.
commonMistakes: Using the terms interchangeably.
Assuming login grants all access.
Relying on hidden UI controls.
Trusting client-sent roles.

vi.title: Authentication và authorization
vi.displayQuestion: Authentication và authorization khác nhau như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra nền tảng về identity và permission.
guidance: Nghe ý bạn là ai so với bạn được làm gì.
Yêu cầu ví dụ ở mức route.
Xác nhận authorization được enforce ở server.
commonMistakes: Dùng hai thuật ngữ thay thế nhau.
Tưởng login là có mọi quyền.
Dựa vào UI bị ẩn.
Tin role client gửi lên.

ja.title: authentication と authorization
ja.displayQuestion: authentication と authorization の違いは何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: identity と permission の基礎を確認する。
guidance: who you are と what you can do の違いに触れるか確認する。
route-level の例を求める。
authorization が server で enforce されることを確認する。
commonMistakes: 2 つの用語を同じ意味で使う。
login すれば全アクセスが許可されると思う。
hidden UI control に頼る。
client が送った role を信頼する。

sourceReferences.note: Used for authentication terminology and controls.

sourceReferences.label: OWASP Authorization Cheat Sheet

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands why dependency maintenance matters in Node.js projects and how to update safely.
primaryQuestion: Why should dependencies be kept updated?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that dependencies can contain known security vulnerabilities, bugs, performance issues, or compatibility problems that are fixed in later versions.

requirements.description: Mentions vulnerabilities, bugs, performance issues, or compatibility fixes.
Explains that later versions may contain fixes.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions that Node.js applications often rely on many transitive dependencies, so vulnerability exposure is not limited to packages directly imported in code.

requirements.description: Mentions transitive dependencies.
Explains vulnerability exposure is not limited to direct imports.

expectedSignals.label: Recognizes that updates should be managed deliberately using lockfiles, changelogs, tests, code review, and staged rollout rather than blindly upgrading production.

requirements.description: Mentions lockfiles, changelogs, tests, or code review.
Mentions staged rollout or avoiding blind production upgrades.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions tooling such as npm audit, npm outdated, Dependabot, Renovate, Snyk, or CI checks to detect vulnerable and outdated packages.

requirements.description: Names tools such as npm audit, npm outdated, Dependabot, Renovate, or Snyk.
Mentions CI checks for vulnerable or outdated packages.

expectedSignals.label: Understands semantic versioning and the difference between patch, minor, and major updates at a basic level.

requirements.description: Mentions semantic versioning.
Distinguishes patch, minor, and major update risk at a basic level.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions removing unused dependencies because every dependency increases maintenance and attack surface.

requirements.description: Mentions removing unused dependencies.
Explains every dependency adds maintenance burden and attack surface.

scoringHints.description: Explains vulnerabilities, bugs, transitive dependencies, safe update workflow, audit tooling, semver, lockfiles, tests, and unused dependency cleanup.
Understands updates matter for security and bugs but misses safe rollout, transitive dependencies, or lockfile handling.
Knows updates are good but cannot describe how to do them responsibly in a Node.js project.
Answer would leave known vulnerable packages untriaged or introduce risky untested upgrades.

followUps.question: What is the role of package-lock.json or another lockfile when updating dependencies?
followUps.purpose: Check reproducible install and dependency resolution awareness.

en.title: Keeping dependencies updated
en.displayQuestion: Why should dependencies be kept updated?
en.displayIntent: Check dependency maintenance and supply chain security basics.
guidance: Listen for known vulnerability and bug fixes.
Ask about transitive dependencies and lockfiles.
Check safe update workflow with tests and review.
commonMistakes: Never updating working packages.
Blindly upgrading to latest in production.
Ignoring transitive dependencies.
Not respecting lockfiles.

vi.title: Cập nhật dependencies
vi.displayQuestion: Tại sao nên giữ dependencies được cập nhật?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra nền tảng dependency maintenance và supply chain security.
guidance: Nghe ý fix vulnerability và bug đã biết.
Hỏi về transitive dependencies và lockfile.
Kiểm tra workflow update an toàn với test và review.
commonMistakes: Không bao giờ update package đã chạy được.
Blindly upgrade latest trong production.
Bỏ qua transitive dependencies.
Không tôn trọng lockfile.

ja.title: dependencies の更新
ja.displayQuestion: dependencies を最新に保つべきなのはなぜですか。
ja.displayIntent: dependency maintenance と supply chain security の基礎を確認する。
guidance: known vulnerability や bug fix に触れるか確認する。
transitive dependencies と lockfile について質問する。
tests と review を含む安全な update workflow を確認する。
commonMistakes: 動いている package を一切更新しない。
production で blindly latest に上げる。
transitive dependencies を無視する。
lockfile を尊重しない。

sourceReferences.label: npm docs: npm audit
sourceReferences.note: Used for npm vulnerability audit workflow.

sourceReferences.label: OWASP Top 10: Vulnerable and Outdated Components
sourceReferences.note: Used for dependency risk framing.

intent: Check whether the candidate can describe a consistent and safe API error response contract for Node.js services.
primaryQuestion: What should a consistent API error response include?

expectedSignals.label: Mentions an appropriate HTTP status code that matches the failure category, such as 400 validation error, 401 unauthenticated, 403 forbidden, 404 not found, or 500 internal error.

requirements.description: Mentions HTTP status codes that match the failure category.
Maps examples such as 400, 401, 403, 404, or 500 to common error cases.

expectedSignals.label: Includes a stable machine-readable error code that clients can use without parsing natural language messages.

requirements.description: Includes a stable machine-readable error code.
Explains clients should not need to parse natural language messages.

expectedSignals.label: Includes a safe human-readable message that explains the problem without exposing stack traces, SQL, secrets, or internal implementation details.

requirements.description: Includes a safe human-readable message.
Avoids exposing stack traces, SQL, secrets, or internal implementation details.

expectedSignals.label: For validation errors, includes structured field-level details such as field, issue, and expected constraint when safe and useful.

requirements.description: Includes structured field-level validation details.
Mentions field, issue, expected constraint, or similar safe details.

expectedSignals.label: Includes a request ID, correlation ID, or trace ID so support and logs can connect the client-visible error to internal diagnostics.

requirements.description: Includes request ID, correlation ID, or trace ID.
Explains the ID links client-visible errors to internal diagnostics/logs.

expectedSignals.label: Mentions consistent JSON shape, content type, and naming across endpoints so frontend and API clients can handle errors uniformly.

requirements.description: Mentions a consistent JSON error shape.
Mentions content type, naming consistency, or uniform client handling across endpoints.

expectedSignals.label: Understands that internal logs should contain richer diagnostic data than public responses.

requirements.description: Separates public error responses from internal diagnostic logs.
Mentions protected logs may contain richer diagnostic details than the client response.

scoringHints.description: Covers status codes, stable error codes, safe messages, validation details, request correlation, consistent JSON shape, and separation from internal logs.
Describes a useful error shape but misses one element such as request ID, validation details, or stable codes.
Knows errors need a message but lacks a consistent contract clients can depend on.
Answer would leak internals or make client-side error handling unreliable.

followUps.question: Can you sketch a JSON response for a validation error where email is missing?
followUps.purpose: Force a concrete response contract.

followUps.question: Why is a stable error code useful even when the message is already clear?
followUps.purpose: Check client contract and localization awareness.

en.title: Consistent API error responses
en.displayQuestion: What should a consistent API error response include?
en.displayIntent: Check safe and client-friendly API error contract design.
guidance: Listen for HTTP status, stable code, safe message, and request ID.
Ask about structured validation errors.
Confirm internal diagnostics stay in logs.
commonMistakes: Returning raw Error objects.
Using different shapes per endpoint.
Always returning HTTP 200.
Leaving out stable codes or request IDs.

vi.title: API error response nhất quán
vi.displayQuestion: Một API error response nhất quán nên gồm những gì?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra thiết kế error contract an toàn và dễ dùng cho client.
guidance: Nghe HTTP status, stable code, safe message va request ID.
Hỏi về validation error có cấu trúc.
Xác nhận diagnostic nội bộ nằm trong log.
commonMistakes: Trả raw Error object.
Mỗi endpoint trả một shape khác nhau.
Luôn trả HTTP 200.
Thiếu stable code hoặc request ID.

ja.title: 一貫した API error response
ja.displayQuestion: 一貫した API error response には何を含めるべきですか。
ja.displayIntent: 安全で client-friendly な API error contract design を確認する。
guidance: HTTP status、stable code、安全な message、request ID に触れるか確認する。
structured validation error について質問する。
internal diagnostics は logs に残すことを確認する。
commonMistakes: raw Error object を返す。
endpoint ごとに異なる shape を返す。
常に HTTP 200 を返す。
stable code や request ID がない。

sourceReferences.label: RFC 7807: Problem Details for HTTP APIs
sourceReferences.note: Used for standardized HTTP API error response concepts.

sourceReferences.label: OWASP Error Handling Cheat Sheet
sourceReferences.note: Used for safe public error response guidance.
